channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tiger Woods prioritises privacy amid DUI arrest following SUV crash
item.description: Golf legend Tiger Woods, aged 50, was arrested for driving under the influence after flipping his Range Rover while attempting to overtake a truck and trailer at high speed on a residential road in Ju...

item.title: Government proposes raising free basic electricity for low-income households to 200kWh
item.description: The South African Ministry of Electricity and Energy is considering increasing the free basic electricity allocation for indigent households from 50kWh to 200kWh per month. Director-General Subesh Pil...

item.title: 16-year-old Chloe Watson dies following serious injuries in Leeds; four held by police
item.description: Chloe Watson, a 16-year-old girl from Cleckheaton, tragically died after being found with severe injuries on Kennerleigh Avenue in the Austhorpe area of Leeds early on Saturday morning. West Yorkshire...

item.title: Rapid growth in Masii town outpaces infrastructure and sanitation services
item.description: Masii, a town in Machakos County, has changed drastically over the past few years, moving from a rural trading post into one of the fastest developing urban centres in the region. The growth has been ...

item.title: Australian government halves fuel excise to ease costs amid global fuel concerns
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has announced a temporary halving of the fuel excise, which currently sits at 52.6 cents per litre, for a three-month period starting Wednesday. This move is part of a ...

item.title: How Winnie Wadera is helping young mothers reclaim their future in Siaya
item.description: Winnie Wadera has become a strong voice in the fight against teenage pregnancy in Kenya, especially in her home area of Siaya County. Through her community initiative, she supports young girls who hav...

item.title: RSU Professor Highlights Key Challenges Affecting Road Construction in Nigeria
item.description: During the 128th inaugural lecture series at Rivers State University, Prof. Emmanuel Osilemme Ekwulo, a Highway Engineering expert, pinpointed the major issues undermining Nigeria’s road construction ...

item.title: London property market sees significant declines as high-end homes sell below purchase price
item.description: London's property market is experiencing a notable downturn, with some homes selling for substantially less than their original purchase price. Factors such as rising interest rates and steep stamp du...

item.title: ESUT urges new students to uphold academic integrity and pass plagiarism checks
item.description: For Enugu State University of Science and Technology (ESUT), the Director of the Institute for Peace, Conflict and Development Studies, Professor Gerald Ezirim, don yan new students say make dem dey c...

item.title: Decline in Maoist Violence in India, Yet Red Corridor Challenges Persist
item.description: According to recent reports, incidents of Maoist-related violence in India have seen a significant decline, with deaths dropping by 82% and overall violence reduced by 90% compared to previous years. ...

item.title: Prairie Harm Reduction faces temporary suspension of supervised consumption site over financial issues
item.description: Prairie Harm Reduction (PHR), the organization operating a supervised consumption site in Saskatoon, has had its exemption to operate temporarily suspended by Health Canada. This action follows the re...

item.title: PMD forecasts rain in Karachi from April 2 to 3 amid westerly system
item.description: Karachi is all set to witness rain on April 2 and 3, according to the Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD), as a westerly weather system approaches the region. The PMD has said that the city will ...

item.title: Manila Churches See Heavy Attendance on Holy Monday; MPD on Full Alert for Visita Iglesia
item.description: On Holy Monday, thousands of Filipino Catholics participated in Visita Iglesia, a traditional practice of visiting multiple churches during Holy Week, particularly in Manila. The streets and major chu...

item.title: Tanzania Plans to Boost Coal Use to Support Manufacturing Growth
item.description: Dodoma – Tanzania dey push hard for industrial growth, and dis one go sharply increase demand for coal. Mining Minister Anthony Mavunde talk say coal go play central role for powering local manufactur...

item.title: Kuwait Confirms Uninterrupted Supply of Diesel and Jet Fuel to Pakistan Amid Strait of Hormuz Closure
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Kuwait has formally assured Pakistan of full facilitation in the supply of diesel and jet fuel, which had been disrupted due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz amid the ongoing US-Israe...

item.title: Understanding Why You Feel Pressured Even When Everything Seems Fine
item.description: Sometimes we feel pressured or stressed even when nothing is actively wrong around us. This happens because our mind keeps juggling a lot of small unfinished tasks at the same time. Even simple things...

item.title: Rights Activist Alleges Police Abduction and Extortion in Anambra State
item.description: Na serious wahala dey happen for Anambra State as rights activist, Harrison Gwamnishu, don raise alarm say some police officers from the Anti-Cultism Unit for Enugu-Ukwu dey abduct, beat, and force pe...

item.title: Parliament Discusses CAPF Bill and Insolvency Code Amendments During Budget Session
item.description: On 30th March 2026, the Indian Parliament was engaged in deliberations over two significant legislative matters during the second part of the Budget session. The Lok Sabha passed the Insolvency and Ba...

item.title: Albanese calls for clarity on US objectives in Iran as Trump signals escalation and possible resource seizure
item.description: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has called for clearer direction from United States President Donald Trump regarding the objectives of the ongoing conflict in Iran, urging de-escalation ami...

item.title: Don Farrell advances discussions on increasing ACT Senate representation
item.description: Federal Labor senator and Special Minister of State Don Farrell has indicated that the ACT may soon gain extra Senate representation, describing the expansion of Parliament as characteristic of 'great...
